info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the \"Info and Links\".

q1.prompt: A patient presents with a chronic, "cauliflower-like" skin lesion on the lower extremity. Histopathological examination of a tissue biopsy reveals chestnut-brown, thick-walled, muriform cells. Which of the following conditions is most likely represented by these findings?

q2.prompt: A public health officer is analyzing the epidemiology of Human African Trypanosomiasis. Which of the following best describes the primary reservoir for the acute, rapidly progressive form of the disease caused by Trypanosoma brucei rhodesiense?

q3.prompt: Several individuals develop watery diarrhea and nausea after consuming imported raspberries. A laboratory investigation identifies acid-fast oocysts in the stool. Which of the following is the drug of choice for this Cyclospora cayetanensis infection?

q4.prompt: A patient from Southeast Asia presents with an ocular infection. Surgical exploration of the periorbital tissue reveals a white, undulating, ribbon-like larval form identified as a "sparganum." Which of the following parasites is the most likely cause?

q5.prompt: A stool specimen from a patient with chronic diarrhea is stained using the modified acid-fast technique. The laboratory technician observes spherical oocysts measuring approximately 8 to 10 µm in diameter. Based on this size, which organism is most likely present?

q6.prompt: A 60-year-old patient with a history of chronic Schistosoma haematobium infection develops progressive shortness of breath. Which of the following vascular conditions is specifically associated with the long-term granulomatous inflammation and fibrosis caused by this parasite?
